steps.title: Use angle sum to find angle C
steps.content:
In any triangle, the sum of angles is 180°. In triangle ABC:
$$18° + 90° + \text{angle } C = 180°$$
$$\text{angle } C = 72°$$

steps.title: Find angle F using congruence
steps.content: Since the triangles are congruent with A↔D and B↔E, the remaining vertices must correspond: C↔F. Congruent triangles have equal corresponding angles, so angle F = angle C = 72°. That matches choice B — B is correct.
